title: Constructor names should start with an upper case letter
description: Constructor names should start with an upper case letter. By convention, constructor function names should start with an upper case letter as a reminder that they should be called with the new keyword. A function is considered to be a constructor when it sets all of its arguments as object properties, and returns no value.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant: function person (firstName, lastName) { this.firstName = firstName; this.lastName = lastName; }
const car = function(make, model) { this.make = make; this.model = model; };
compliant: function Person (firstName, lastName) { this.firstName = firstName; this.lastName = lastName; }
const Car = function(make, model) { this.make = make; this.model = model; };

checks.message: Constructor function name should start with an uppercase letter.
Constructor function variable should start with an uppercase letter.
Constructor name in 'new' statement should start with an uppercase letter.
